hellMaidenBeginnerGuide.title: Hell Maiden Beginner Guide
hellMaidenBeginnerGuide.intro: Hell Maiden combines automatic attacks with manual aiming and active dodging. Keep moving, collect experience, build a four-Weapon hand, strengthen those weapons with Mod Cards, and preserve enough health and mobility for the boss encounter after the survival timer.

steps.title: Move and Aim at the Same Time
steps.description: Dante attacks automatically, but you control the direction of her attacks with the mouse cursor or controller aim input. Circle around enemy groups and point attacks into the densest part of each wave instead of standing still.
steps.priority: Keep enemies on one side of Dante so experience drops remain easy to collect.

steps.title: Use the Dash to Cross Enemy Lines
steps.description: The dash lets Dante pass through dangerous gaps without taking damage. Use it to escape surrounding enemies, cross projectile patterns, or leave a Temple area after activating it.
steps.priority: Do not spend every dash on normal movement; keep one available for elites and boss telegraphs.

steps.title: Collect Experience and Choose Cards
steps.description: Defeated enemies drop experience orbs that increase Dante's level. Level-up choices can add Weapon Cards, attach Mod Cards, or strengthen effects already present in the hand.
steps.priority: Collect nearby experience regularly instead of leaving large groups of orbs behind the enemy wave.

steps.title: Build a Four-Weapon Hand
steps.description: Your hand can hold four Weapon Cards. Fill the empty slots while keeping one preferred weapon as the main damage source, then attach Mods that improve damage, projectile count, attack speed, status effects, or survivability.
steps.priority: Avoid filling every slot with unrelated effects before your main weapon has useful Mods.

steps.title: Merge Duplicate Mod Cards
steps.description: Identical Mod Cards can be combined into stronger versions, with Level 3 serving as an important build milestone. Some Mods also strengthen weapons according to their position, so rearrange the hand whenever a positional effect changes.
steps.priority: Merge duplicates on the weapon doing the most damage rather than spreading weak copies across the entire hand.

steps.title: Activate Temples During the Run
steps.description: Temples provide additional run progression and appear throughout each Circle. Explore while the waves are manageable, activate different Temple types, and work toward activating 12 Temples in a single run.
steps.priority: Visit Temples early and mid-run; late waves make long detours much more dangerous.

steps.title: Survive Limbo and Defeat Queen Minos
steps.description: Circle 1 requires Dante to survive for 12 minutes before its final encounter. Enter the boss sequence with a complete Weapon hand, upgraded Mods, a ready Spell, and enough health to handle Queen Minos's projectile patterns and telegraphed area attacks.
steps.priority: First-session route: collect experience, fill Weapon slots, focus one Level 3 Mod stack, activate nearby Temples, rescue available Poets, survive 12 minutes, then use dashes and Dante's Spell during Queen Minos's most restrictive patterns.

quickTips: Dante attacks automatically — your job is aiming, dodging, and positioning.
Fill all four Weapon slots early so every level-up can add Mods instead of empty slots.
Merge duplicate Mod Cards toward Level 3 on your strongest Weapon.
Activate Temples early and mid-run while waves are still manageable.
Rescue every Poet you can reach to expand your permanent card pool.

hellMaidenBestBuilds.title: Hell Maiden Best Builds and Spirit Cards
hellMaidenBestBuilds.intro: Spirit Cards are divided into Weapon Cards and Mod Cards. A dependable build normally concentrates its strongest upgrades on one signature Weapon, uses the remaining slots for complementary attacks, and merges duplicate Mods toward Level 3 instead of collecting many weak effects.

builds.name: Focused Damage Build
builds.bestFor: Direct damage, elites, and reliable Limbo clears
builds.positioning: Place the core Weapon beside any Mod or Weapon effect that strengthens adjacent cards or cards positioned to its right.
builds.buildPlan: Choose one core Weapon early, merge its duplicate Mods, and work toward equipping three Level 3 Mods on that Weapon. Use secondary Weapon slots for coverage rather than competing damage investments.
builds.spellUsage: Save Dante's Inferno or Horace's Spell for dense waves and boss phases that restrict movement.

builds.name: Projectile Pressure Build
builds.bestFor: Large enemy groups and continuous screen coverage
builds.positioning: Use the two middle Weapon slots for cards with adjacent bonuses so one positional effect can support more than one attack.
builds.buildPlan: Increase the number and frequency of attacks first, then add damage after the screen is consistently covered. Merge duplicate projectile Mods instead of placing low-level copies on every Weapon.
builds.spellUsage: Virgil's Star Sign Pleiades provides a powerful emergency attack when normal projectiles cannot open a safe path.

builds.name: Status Control Build
builds.bestFor: Controlling fast waves while dealing damage through repeated hits
builds.positioning: Attach status-focused Mods to fast or multi-hit Weapons and keep one separate Weapon dedicated to immediate damage.
builds.buildPlan: Spread status application across two Weapons, but concentrate raw damage Mods on a single finisher. This prevents the hand from becoming dependent on effects that take time to build.
builds.spellUsage: Use a Poet's Spell after status effects have gathered enemies or during an elite wave that is difficult to contain.

builds.name: Survival Clear Build
builds.bestFor: First clears, Temple exploration, and low-healing challenges
builds.positioning: Keep the safest crowd-clearing Weapon in the main supported position and use secondary slots for protection and coverage.
builds.buildPlan: Secure consistent area damage before investing fully in boss damage. Visit Temples while enemy pressure is low, avoid unnecessary contact damage, and preserve the dash for enclosed situations.
builds.spellUsage: Treat the Spell as a defensive reset when Dante is surrounded rather than using it immediately after it becomes available.

builds.name: Boss-Focused Build
builds.bestFor: Queen Minos, Scarmiglione, Libicocco, and other high-health targets
builds.positioning: Give the core boss Weapon the strongest positional bonuses and keep crowd-clearing Weapons in the remaining slots for summoned enemies.
builds.buildPlan: Winged Lyric attacks one target at a time without missing, making it suited to sustained pressure on elites and bosses. Codex Amoris supports Ovid-focused Lust clears, while Blazing Quill can complete both available Circles.
builds.spellUsage: Wait for the boss to finish a movement-heavy pattern, then activate the signature Spell while the target remains inside the attack area.

hellMaidenWalkthrough.title: Hell Maiden Limbo and Lust Walkthrough
hellMaidenWalkthrough.intro: Every Circle combines enemy waves, quests, Temples, Poet rescues, card building, and a final boss challenge. Complete optional objectives while waves are still manageable, then stop taking long detours once your hand is ready for the final minutes.

stages.title: Circle 1: Limbo
objectives: Collect experience and fill all four Weapon slots
Complete Limbo quests and rescue Horace and Homer
stages.recommendedSetup: Use Blazing Quill, Wayfinder, Carpe Diem, or Winged Lyric as the core Weapon. Enter the boss encounter with at least one heavily upgraded Weapon, useful crowd coverage, and a ready Spell.
stages.bossTips: Queen Minos uses bullet-hell projectiles and telegraphed area attacks. Move around the edge of dangerous zones, dash only when a safe lane closes, and activate the Spell during patterns that force enemies and projectiles into a narrow area.
stages.rewards: Clearing Limbo advances the story, opens further Poet dialogue, supports the rescue of Horace and Homer, and leads toward unlocking Circle 2.

stages.title: Unlock Circle 2: Lust
requirements: Continue the Forum story and Poet interactions
Select Lust after the Winds of Desire progression unlock
stages.preparation: Use additional runs in Limbo to unlock more Poet cards, complete dialogue, improve familiarity with positional Mods, and practice reaching the boss with limited healing.
stages.recommendedGoal: Build a complete Weapon and Mod hand before entering Lust so its faster enemies and elite waves do not interrupt early deck development.
stages.bossTips: Treat extra Limbo clears as practice for arriving at a boss with a fully developed hand and limited healing resources.
stages.rewards: Unlocking Lust grants Winds of Desire and opens the second Circle's Poet, enemies, and bosses.

stages.title: Circle 2: Lust
objectives: Stabilize the build before elite Vipers and Succubi appear
Continue activating Temples without becoming surrounded
Rescue Ovid and unlock the Codex Amoris card line
Defeat the Libicocco and Scarmiglione encounter
stages.recommendedSetup: Bring a combination of crowd control and focused boss damage. Projectile or status builds help manage Lust's dense waves, while a Level 3 Mod stack and saved Spell provide the damage needed for its final encounters.
stages.bossTips: Keep moving laterally during telegraphed attacks, clear summoned threats before they block escape routes, and avoid using every dash during the opening pattern. Use the signature Spell once the boss pair is grouped or committed to an attack.
stages.rewards: Lust progression adds Ovid, Codex Amoris, further story chapters, additional dialogue, and abilities earned from the Circle's defeated bosses.

hellMaidenCharacters.title: Hell Maiden Poets of Limbo and Character Unlocks
hellMaidenCharacters.intro: Hell Maiden continues the story of Dante's Divine Comedy after Dante reaches Paradiso and mysteriously returns to Hell without her memories. Virgil guides her, Beatrice protects her, and the Poets of Limbo provide new combat options as Dante helps them pursue salvation.

characters.name: Dante Alighieri
characters.story: Dante returns to Hell after reaching Paradiso and has lost the memories of her previous journey. She begins climbing through the Circles again in search of answers and another path to Heaven.

characters.name: Virgil
characters.story: The Roman poet accompanies Dante through Hell and serves as her guide through the abyss. Virgil is one of the central Poets connected to Dante's early progression.
characters.unlock: Progress through the opening story and interact with Virgil in the Poets' Forum

characters.name: Beatrice
characters.story: Beatrice protects Dante during her renewed journey through Hell and remains connected to Dante's search for Paradiso and her missing memories.
rewards: Progress toward the Pilgrim's Journey interaction objective

characters.name: Horace
characters.story: Horace is one of the first Poets Dante can rescue while exploring Limbo. Saving her expands the available card pool and opens further interactions in the Forum.
rewards: Blessing, perk, Spell, and ultimate attack

characters.name: Homer
characters.story: Homer is another Poet rescued during Limbo progression. Her signature Weapon focuses its attack on a single target and does not miss.
rewards: Blessing, perk, Spell, and ultimate attack

characters.name: Ovid
characters.story: Ovid becomes part of Dante's growing group of Poets during Circle 2 progression and provides a new set of cards suited to later runs.
characters.unlock: Rescue Ovid during Circle 2, Lust progression
rewards: Blessing, perk, Spell, and ultimate attack

characters.name: Lucan
characters.story: Lucan is named among the legendary poets and artists Dante will encounter during the wider journey through the nine Circles of Hell.
characters.unlock: Connected to the expanded Early Access roadmap and future Circle progression
characters.signatureWeapon: To be introduced with later Poet content
characters.spell: To be introduced with later Poet content

hellMaidenBosses.title: Hell Maiden Bosses and Elite Enemies
hellMaidenBosses.intro: Early Access includes two complete Circles with major boss encounters, named elite targets, and distinct enemy families. Boss victories expand the ability pool, while elite kills and repeated clears unlock dedicated Steam achievements.

encounters.name: Queen Minos
encounters.attackPattern: Queen Minos cycles through several clearly telegraphed arena attacks. The launch hotfix reduced how often she can repeat the same move in quick succession, so players must read a changing sequence instead of relying on one fixed loop.
encounters.recommendedCounter: Keep moving around the outer part of the arena, preserve the dash for committed attacks, and deal damage during the recovery window after each telegraph. Avoid spending every escape option while normal enemies or projectiles are still occupying the route ahead.
encounters.preparation: Enter with a complete Weapon and Mod setup, at least one reliable area-clearing weapon, upgraded damage Mods, and a Poet Spell ready for a crowded or unsafe phase.
encounters.victoryReward: Defeating Queen Minos unlocks Soul Magister. Three separate victories unlock Taboo Arts I, and major boss wins add two new abilities to the permanent card pool.

encounters.name: Scarmiglione
encounters.attackPattern: Scarmiglione applies fast, aggressive pressure and forces frequent repositioning. The safest damage windows appear after a committed approach or attack sequence rather than while the boss is actively closing distance.
encounters.recommendedCounter: Move sideways instead of retreating in a straight line, wait for the attack direction to become clear before dashing, and use burst damage during recovery. Clear nearby enemies first when they block the route needed for the next dodge.
encounters.preparation: Prioritize movement control, dependable single-target damage, a health reserve, Level 3 Mods on the main weapon, and a Poet Spell that can create space or finish a dangerous phase.
encounters.victoryReward: Defeating Scarmiglione unlocks Salamander Inquisitor. Defeating both Scarmiglione and Libicocco unlocks Malebranche Punisher; completing both victories three separate times unlocks Taboo Arts II.

encounters.name: Libicocco
encounters.attackPattern: Libicocco can create clones during the encounter, multiplying visual pressure and making overlapping attacks harder to read. The launch hotfix also fixed a progression issue that could occur after the clone attack.
encounters.recommendedCounter: Keep an open escape lane before the clones appear, avoid standing in the center of overlapping threats, and use wide-area attacks to control copies while sustained damage remains focused on the main encounter.
encounters.preparation: Bring both crowd control and boss damage instead of relying on a purely single-target hand. Preserve enough health and dash availability for the clone phase, and avoid triggering the Poet Spell before the arena becomes crowded.
encounters.victoryReward: Defeating Libicocco and Scarmiglione unlocks Malebranche Punisher. Three complete sets of victories unlock Taboo Arts II, while major boss victories expand the permanent ability pool.

encounters.name: Elite Viper
encounters.attackPattern: The Elite Viper appears as a priority target during Lust's survival waves. It is more dangerous when mixed into a dense horde because nearby enemies reduce the space available for clean movement and focused damage.
encounters.recommendedCounter: Identify it early, move away from the thickest part of the wave, and focus high-damage weapons on the elite while area attacks keep normal enemies from surrounding the player.
encounters.preparation: Use a hand with at least one area weapon and one strong focused-damage option. Extra movement or defensive bonuses make the elite encounter safer when it arrives during a crowded wave.
encounters.victoryReward: Killing the Elite Viper unlocks Elite Killer I. Standard Viper kills also count toward Viper Hunter, which requires 1,000 Viper enemies.

encounters.name: Elite Succubus
encounters.attackPattern: The Elite Succubus is a named high-priority threat inside Lust's enemy waves. Its danger increases when the player remains trapped near other enemies and cannot maintain a clear movement route.
encounters.recommendedCounter: Create distance before committing to damage, avoid fighting inside the center of the horde, and use upgraded projectiles or status effects to maintain pressure while repositioning.
encounters.preparation: Favor steady damage, area coverage, and enough survivability to avoid taking repeated contact damage while isolating the elite from the surrounding wave.
encounters.victoryReward: Killing the Elite Succubus unlocks Elite Killer II. Standard Succubus kills count toward Succubi Hunter, which requires 250 kills in Circle 2, Lust.

encounters.name: Circle Enemy Families
encounters.attackPattern: Achievement targets confirm Imp, Skeleton, Brotchi, Ghoul, Lost Soul, Slime, Lust Sinner, Lust Mine, Viper, Devi-Brotchi, and Succubus enemy families across the available content.
encounters.recommendedCounter: Use overlapping area damage for dense melee groups, projectiles for targets entering from multiple directions, and frequent route changes before the horde closes around the player.
encounters.preparation: Build a balanced hand instead of stacking only boss damage. One crowd-clearing weapon, one focused-damage weapon, useful Mods, and an emergency Poet Spell provide dependable coverage.
encounters.victoryReward: Each enemy family has a cumulative hunting achievement, with targets ranging from 250 Succubi to 2,000 Brotchi or Lust Sinners.

hellMaidenProgression.title: Hell Maiden Progression and Resources
hellMaidenProgression.intro: Progression combines in-run leveling and deck building with Early Access meta-progression. The strongest route is to unlock Poets and their cards first, clear each Circle consistently, then chase rare Charms, high scores, complete card collections, and challenge achievements.

rows.howItWorks: Temples are activated while exploring a Circle and provide run-based benefits. The achievement list confirms multiple Temple types and enough distinct Temples to activate 12 in one run.
rows.milestone: Activate all available Temple types for Devout Pilgrim and 12 different Temples in one run for Pious Pilgrim.

rows.howItWorks: Charms provide rarity-based bonuses that strengthen a run. Crystal is a confirmed rarity tier and is tied to one of the rarer progression achievements.
rows.milestone: Obtain a Crystal-rarity Charm to unlock Heavenly Gift.

rows.howItWorks: Wails of the Abyss are a spendable progression resource used between runs. They support the expanded meta-progression system introduced for Early Access.
rows.priority: Spend on consistent clears before narrow challenges
rows.milestone: Spend a cumulative total of 5,000 Wails of the Abyss to unlock Abyssal Spender.

rows.howItWorks: Collect experience from defeated enemies to level up and choose cards or upgrades. The level resets with the run, so survival and efficient experience collection determine how quickly a build reaches full strength.
rows.priority: Very high in the opening minutes
rows.milestone: Reach Level 50 to unlock Infernal Prowess I.

rows.howItWorks: Score rewards efficient kills, survival, and strong run performance. Limbo and Lust each have a separate 100,000-point mastery achievement.
rows.milestone: Reach 100,000 in Limbo for Hellish Mastery I and 100,000 in Lust for Hellish Mastery II.

rows.howItWorks: Early Access features four Poet characters in its current progression and card systems. Achievement paths explicitly cover Virgil, Horace, Homer, and Ovid, while Horace, Homer, and Ovid have dedicated rescue milestones.
rows.milestone: Rescue Horace, Homer, and Ovid; every rescued Poet adds five new abilities to the permanent pool.

rows.where: Built each run; options expand permanently
rows.howItWorks: The current Early Access pool contains more than 40 abilities across Weapon and Mod choices. Weapon Cards define attacks, while Mod Cards enhance a weapon and can be upgraded to Level 3.
rows.priority: Build one damage core before spreading
rows.milestone: Complete full hands, equip three Level 3 Mods to one Weapon, and collect every Poet's cards.

rows.howItWorks: Major boss victories add new build options to later runs instead of only ending the current Circle.
rows.priority: Repeat boss clears after the first victory
rows.milestone: Each defeated boss adds two new abilities to the permanent pool.

rows.howItWorks: Limbo is the starting Circle. Continued progression opens Lust, the second complete Circle available in Early Access.
rows.milestone: Unlock Circle 2, Lust, to earn Winds of Desire.

rows.howItWorks: First learn Limbo and activate Temples, then rescue Poets, defeat Queen Minos, unlock Lust, and build consistent 12-minute clears. After that, repeat the Malebranche bosses, improve card collections, spend Wails, and pursue score or endurance challenges.
rows.milestone: Limbo clear, Poet rescues, Queen Minos repeats, Lust unlock, Malebranche clears, Level 50 and 100,000-point runs, then full card collections.

hellMaidenAchievements.title: All 72 Hell Maiden Achievements
hellMaidenAchievements.intro: Hell Maiden currently has 72 Steam achievements. The checklist below preserves the official requirements and highlights the long-run goals that usually need a dedicated build, repeated clears, or complete Poet card collections.

achievements.name: Astral Wayfarer
achievements.requirement: Use Blazing Quill in Limbo twice.

achievements.name: Conqueror of Limbo
achievements.requirement: Survive 12 minutes in Circle 1, Limbo.

achievements.name: Golden Satirist
achievements.requirement: Rescue Horace.

achievements.name: Crystal-eyed Bard
achievements.requirement: Rescue Homer.

achievements.name: Poets of Limbo I
achievements.requirement: Save Horace and Homer.

achievements.name: Winds of Desire
achievements.requirement: Unlock Circle 2, Lust.

achievements.name: Infernal Prowess I
achievements.requirement: Reach Level 50.

achievements.name: Conqueror of Lust
achievements.requirement: Survive 12 minutes in Circle 2, Lust.

achievements.name: Cerulean Imago
achievements.requirement: Rescue Ovid.

achievements.name: Poets of Limbo II
achievements.requirement: Save Ovid.

achievements.name: Hellish Mastery I
achievements.requirement: Reach a high score of 100,000 in Circle 1, Limbo.

achievements.name: Hellish Mastery II
achievements.requirement: Reach a high score of 100,000 in Circle 2, Lust.

achievements.name: Heavenly Gift
achievements.requirement: Get a Charm of Crystal rarity.

achievements.name: Abyssal Spender
achievements.requirement: Spend a total of 5,000 Wails of the Abyss.

achievements.name: Weapon Master
achievements.requirement: Get a full hand of Weapon cards in a single run.

achievements.name: Strategy Master
achievements.requirement: Get a full hand of Weapon and Mod cards in a single run.

achievements.name: Mod Master
achievements.requirement: Equip three Level 3 Mod cards onto a single Weapon card.

achievements.name: Perfect Hand
achievements.requirement: Get a full hand of Level 3 Mod cards in a single run.

achievements.name: Soul of Poet I
achievements.requirement: Equip all of Dante's Weapon cards.

achievements.name: Soul of Poet II
achievements.requirement: Equip all of Virgil's Weapon cards.

achievements.name: Soul of Poet III
achievements.requirement: Equip all of Horace's Weapon cards.

achievements.name: Soul of Poet IV
achievements.requirement: Equip all of Homer's Weapon cards.

achievements.name: Soul of Poet V
achievements.requirement: Equip all of Ovid's Weapon cards.

achievements.name: Essence of Poet I
achievements.requirement: Equip all of Dante's Mod cards.

achievements.name: Essence of Poet II
achievements.requirement: Equip all of Virgil's Mod cards.

achievements.name: Essence of Poet III
achievements.requirement: Equip all of Horace's Mod cards.

achievements.name: Essence of Poet IV
achievements.requirement: Equip all of Homer's Mod cards.

achievements.name: Essence of Poet V
achievements.requirement: Equip all of Ovid's Mod cards.

achievements.name: Blazing Quill I
achievements.requirement: Use Dante's Spell.

achievements.name: Blazing Quill II
achievements.requirement: Beat Circle 1, Limbo, using Dante's Blazing Quill.

achievements.name: Blazing Quill III
achievements.requirement: Beat Circle 2, Lust, using Dante's Blazing Quill.

achievements.name: Wayfinder I
achievements.requirement: Use Virgil's Wayfinder.

achievements.name: Wayfinder II
achievements.requirement: Use Virgil's Spell.

achievements.name: Wayfinder III
achievements.requirement: Beat Circle 1, Limbo, using Virgil's Wayfinder.

achievements.name: Carpe Diem I
achievements.requirement: Use Horace's Carpe Diem.

achievements.name: Carpe Diem II
achievements.requirement: Use Horace's Spell.

achievements.name: Carpe Diem III
achievements.requirement: Beat Circle 1, Limbo, using Horace's Carpe Diem.

achievements.name: Winged Lyric I
achievements.requirement: Use Homer's Winged Lyric.

achievements.name: Winged Lyric II
achievements.requirement: Use Homer's Spell.

achievements.name: Winged Lyric III
achievements.requirement: Beat Circle 1, Limbo, using Homer's Winged Lyric.

hellMaidenRoadmap.title: Hell Maiden Early Access Roadmap and Updates
hellMaidenRoadmap.intro: Hell Maiden entered Early Access with two complete Circles, four Poets, four signature weapons, more than 40 abilities, bosses, story content, and permanent progression. AstralShift plans regular fixes and content updates while expanding the game toward all nine Circles.

entries.headline: Hell Maiden Revealed
entries.details: AstralShift publicly introduced Hell Maiden as a horde-survival action roguelite with deck-building progression, Poet companions, story chapters, and an anime-inspired vision of Dante's Inferno.

entries.headline: Playable Demo and First Hotfix
entries.details: The public demo offered the complete Limbo Circle, more than 25 abilities, one Poet, six Weapon Cards, and ten Mod Cards. An early hotfix followed with gameplay and quality-of-life corrections.

entries.headline: Original Demo Removed
entries.details: AstralShift removed the old demo because the Early Access build had diverged through new meta-progression, rebalanced stats, and changed mechanics. The studio plans a new parity demo after the Early Access version stabilizes.

entries.headline: Two Circles Available at Launch
entries.details: Early Access launched with Limbo and Lust, four Poets of Limbo to find and rescue, four signature weapons, more than 40 abilities, major bosses, quests, story content, and permanent progression between runs.

entries.headline: Launch-Day Progression and Balance Fixes
entries.details: The launch hotfix fixed progression stopping after Libicocco's clone attack, corrected interface and localization issues, ensured Curse of Dido grants at least one projectile, reduced Queen Minos repeating the same attacks too quickly, and added a DirectX 11 beta launch option.

entries.headline: Circle 3 — Gluttony
entries.details: The official roadmap targets Gluttony as the next Circle later in 2026, adding another stage of the descent through Hell with new enemies, progression content, and encounters.

entries.headline: Regular Patches and Content Expansion
entries.details: The developers plan frequent hotfixes, balance changes, quality-of-life improvements, new Circles, Poets, cards, bosses, quests, lore, weapons, perks, and ultimate abilities. The Early Access period is expected to continue for roughly one year or longer as development requires.

entries.headline: All Nine Circles and Endless Mode
entries.details: The completed version is planned to include all nine Circles of Hell, a larger roster of Poets, more signature weapons, perks, cards, ultimate attacks, bosses, lore, side quests, quality-of-life improvements, and an Endless Mode. The store page also states that the price is expected to increase at full release.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Hell Maiden

questions.question: What is Hell Maiden?
questions.answer: Hell Maiden is a gothic horde-survival deckbuilding roguelite on Steam. You play as Dante, attacking automatically while aiming, dashing, collecting experience, and building a hand of Spirit Cards across the Circles of Hell.

questions.question: Is Hell Maiden a singleplayer game?
questions.answer: Yes. Hell Maiden is a singleplayer game with no multiplayer or co-op.

questions.question: Where can I play Hell Maiden?
questions.answer: Hell Maiden is available on Steam for PC. It entered Steam Early Access on July 16, 2026.

questions.question: How many Circles are available in Hell Maiden Early Access?
questions.answer: Early Access launched with two complete Circles — Limbo and Lust. The roadmap plans to expand Hell Maiden toward all nine Circles of Hell over time.

questions.question: How many achievements does Hell Maiden have?
questions.answer: Hell Maiden currently has 72 Steam achievements across progression, card collection, Poets, bosses, survival, enemy hunting, and story categories.

questions.question: Does Hell Maiden support controllers?
questions.answer: Yes. Hell Maiden supports mouse, keyboard, and controller aim input. Dante attacks automatically, so you mainly control aiming, dashing, and positioning.

questions.question: Will Hell Maiden keep getting updates?
questions.answer: Yes. AstralShift plans regular hotfixes, balance changes, new Circles, Poets, cards, bosses, and an eventual Endless Mode for Hell Maiden during Early Access and toward full release.

cta.title: Ready to Play Hell Maiden?
cta.description: Join the Hell Maiden community on Discord for build guides, boss strategies, and Early Access updates!

guides.metaDescription: Complete Hell Maiden guides covering beginner tips, Spirit Card decks, boss strategies, Circle walkthroughs, and expert tactics for mastering the gothic deckbuilder.
guides.whatIsDescription: Comprehensive guides covering Hell Maiden gameplay mechanics, aiming and dodging, Spirit Card deck building, and Circle progression from first steps to advanced techniques.

whySteps.step1Description: Understand aiming, dashing, experience collection, and avoid common beginner mistakes
whySteps.step2Description: Follow proven Weapon and Mod Card combinations to survive each Circle
whySteps.step3Description: Develop tactics for Queen Minos, the Malebranche, and elite enemies

crafting.title: Hell Maiden Builds
crafting.description: Master Spirit Card deck building with the strongest Weapon and Mod Card combinations for damage, projectiles, status effects, and boss fights.
crafting.metaTitle: Hell Maiden Builds - Best Deck & Spirit Card Setups
crafting.metaDescription: Master Hell Maiden deck building with the best Weapon and Mod Card combinations. Learn card positioning, merging duplicate Mods, and Level 3 build paths for every playstyle.
crafting.whatIsDescription: Builds combine one core Weapon with complementary attacks and merged duplicate Mods toward Level 3. Each setup covers a different playstyle such as focused damage, projectile pressure, status control, survival, or boss focus.

whySteps.step1Description: Choose one signature Weapon early and concentrate your strongest upgrades on it
whySteps.step2Description: Combine identical Mod Cards toward Level 3 instead of collecting many weak effects
whySteps.step3Description: Arrange the hand so positional bonuses support your main damage source

items.title: Hell Maiden Spirit Cards
items.description: Detailed Spirit Card guides covering Weapon Cards, Mod Cards, effects, merging rules, rarity, and unlock sources.
items.metaTitle: Hell Maiden Spirit Cards - Weapon & Mod Card Database
items.metaDescription: Complete Hell Maiden Spirit Card database including Weapon Cards, Mod Cards, effects, merging rules, rarity, and unlock sources for every Poet.
items.whatIsDescription: Spirit Cards are divided into Weapon Cards that define attacks and Mod Cards that enhance a weapon. Mods can be merged and upgraded to Level 3, and each Poet unlocks their own signature card line.

whySteps.step1Description: Understand what each Weapon and Mod Card does before committing to a build
whySteps.step2Description: Know which duplicate Mods combine toward Level 3 milestones
whySteps.step3Description: Rescue Poets to expand your permanent Weapon and Mod Card pool

biomes.title: Hell Maiden Circles
biomes.description: Explore the Circles of Hell, survival objectives, quests, Temples, Poet rescues, enemy waves, and boss encounters.
biomes.metaTitle: Hell Maiden Circles - Limbo, Lust & Walkthroughs
biomes.metaDescription: Explore Hell Maiden Circles including Limbo and Lust. Find survival objectives, Temples, Poet rescues, enemy families, and boss preparation for each Circle.
biomes.whatIsDescription: Each Circle is a 12-minute survival run combining enemy waves, quests, Temples, Poet rescues, card building, and a final boss. Limbo and Lust are available in Early Access, with more Circles planned on the roadmap.

whySteps.step1Description: Finish quests, rescue Poets, and activate Temples while waves are manageable
whySteps.step2Description: Build a complete hand before the final boss encounter begins
whySteps.step3Description: Prepare recommended setups and learn each boss's attack patterns

building.title: Hell Maiden Characters
building.description: Meet Dante, Virgil, Beatrice, and the Poets of Limbo. Learn their roles, stories, signature weapons, Spells, and unlock methods.
building.metaTitle: Hell Maiden Characters - Poets, Weapons & Unlocks
building.metaDescription: Complete Hell Maiden character guide covering Dante, Virgil, Beatrice, Horace, Homer, Ovid, and the Poets of Limbo with their signature weapons, Spells, and unlock methods.
building.whatIsDescription: Hell Maiden follows Dante's renewed journey through Hell alongside Virgil and Beatrice. Rescuing the Poets of Limbo unlocks signature Weapons, Blessings, perks, Spells, ultimate attacks, and new dialogue.

whySteps.step1Description: Each rescued Poet adds five new abilities and a signature Weapon line
whySteps.step2Description: Gain emergency Spells for dense waves and dangerous boss phases
whySteps.step3Description: Open new Forum dialogue and salvation-related story chapters
